handling and user-friendly error messages.\n69\t// Each variant represents a distinct category of parsing failure.\n70\ttype ParseErrorVariant string\n71\t\n72\tconst (\n73\t\t// ParseErrorVariantSyntax represents YAML syntax errors.\n74\t\t//\n75\t\t// This variant covers fundamental YAML syntax issues including:\n76\t\t// - Invalid indentation\n77\t\t// - Malformed YAML structure\n78\t\t// - Invalid escape sequences\n79\t\t// - Improper quoting\n80\t\t// - Invalid YAML tokens\n81\t\tParseErrorVariantSyntax ParseErrorVariant = \"syntax\"\n82\t\n83\t\t// ParseErrorVariantTypeMismatch represents type conversion errors.\n84\t\t//\n85\t\t// This variant covers errors when YAML content cannot be converted to the\n86\t\t// expected Go type, ve.Type\n341\t\t}\n342\t\t// Try to determine error type from error code or message\n343\t\tif ve.ErrorCode == ErrCodeRequiredField || (ve.FieldPath != \"\" &&   ErrorTypeValidation,\n447\t\t}\n448\t}\n449\t\n450\t// IsValidationError checks if an error is a ValidationError.\n451\tfunc IsValidationError(err error) bool {\n452\t\tvar ve initialization.\n457\tfunc NewFileError(path string, operation string, message string, errorCode ErrorCode) *FileError {\n458\t\treturn &FileError{\n459\t\t\tPath:       path,\n460\t\t\tOperation: creates a new DuplicateKeyError with proper initialization.\n542\t//\n543\t// This constructor function provides a convenient way to create properly initialized\n544\t// DuplicateKeyError instances with the given parameters.\n545\t//\n546\t// Parameters:\n547\t//   - filePath: Path to the file with duplicate keys\n548\t//   - key: The duplicate key name\n549\t//   - location: Nested path to the duplicate key (optional, use empty string if not applicable)\n550\t//   - line1: Line number of first occurrence (use 0 if unknown)\n551\t//   - line2: Line number of duplicate occurrence (use 0 if unknown)\n552\t//   - code: Error code for programmatic handling (use empty string for default)\n553\t//\n554\t// Returns a properly initialized DuplicateKeyError that implements the YAMLError interface.\n555\tfunc NewDuplicateKeyError(filePath string, key string, location string, line1 int, line2 int, code ErrorCode) *DuplicateKeyError {\n556\t\t// Use provided code or default to duplicate key error\n557\t\terrorCode := code\n558\t\tif errorCode == \"\" {\n559\t\t\terrorCode = ErrCodeDuplicateKey\n560\t\t}\n561\t\n562\t\treturn &DuplicateKeyError{\n563\t\t\tFilePath:  filePath,\n564\t\t\tKey:       key,\n565\t\t\tLocation:  location,\n566\t\t\tLine1:     line1,\n567\t\t\tLine2:     line2,\n568\t\t\tErrorCode: errorCode,\n569\t\t}\n570\t}\n571\t\n572\t// NewSchemaLoadError creates a new SchemaLoadError with proper initialization.\n573\t//\n574\t// This constructor function provides a convenient way to create properly initialized\n575\t// SchemaLoadError instances with the given parameters.\n576\t//\n577\t// Parameters:\n578\t//   - filePath: Path to the schema file\n579\t//   - message: Description of the load error\n580\t//   - err: Underlying error (optional, use nil if not applicable)\n581\t//   - code: Error code for programmatic handling (use empty string for default)\n582\t//\n583\t// Returns a properly initialized SchemaLoadError that implements the YAMLError interface.\n584\tfunc NewSchemaLoadError(filePath string, message string, err error, code ErrorCode) *SchemaLoadError {\n585\t\t// Use provided code or default to schema load failed error\n586\t\terrorCode := code\n587\t\tif errorCode == \"\" {\n588\t\t\terrorCode = ErrCodeSchemaLoadFailed\n589\t\t}\n590\t\n591\t\treturn &SchemaLoadError{\n592\t\t\tFilePath:  filePath,\n593\t\t\tMessage:   message,\n594\t\t\tErr:       err,\n595\t\t\tErrorCode: errorCode,\n596\t\t}\n597\t}\n598\t\n599\t// containsRequiredFieldKeywords checks if the message suggests a required field error.\n600\tfunc containsRequiredFieldKeywords(msg string) bool {\n601\t\tkeywords := []string{\"required\", \"missing\", \"not found\", \"must be provided\"}\n602\t\tlower := strings.ToLower(msg)\n603\t\tfor _, kw := range keywords {\n604\t\t\tif strings.Contains(lower, kw) {\n605\t\t\t\treturn true\n606\t\t\t}\n607\t\t}\n608\t\treturn false\n609\t}\n610\t\n611\t// containsConstraintKeywords checks if the message suggests a constraint violation.\n612\tfunc containsConstraintKeywords(msg string) bool {\n613\t\tkeywords := []string{\"constraint\", \"range\", \"length\", \"pattern\", \"invalid value\"}\n614\t\tlower := strings.ToLower(msg)\n615\t\tfor _, kw := range keywords {\n616\t\t\tif strings.Contains(lower, kw) {\n617\t\t\t\treturn true\n618\t\t\t}\n619\t\t}\n620\t\treturn false\n621\t}\n622\t\n623\t// FileError represents errors that occur during file I/O operations.\n624\t//\n625\t// FileError wraps file I/O errors and provides detailed\n626\t// information about the file operation that failed.\n627\ttype FileError struct {\n628\t\tOp        string    // Operation that failed (read, write, etc.) - \"Op\" for backward compatibility\n629\t\tPath      string    // Path to the file\n630\t\tOperation string    // Operation that failed (read, write, etc.) - \"Operation\" for clarity\n631\t\tMessage   string    // Human-readable error message\n632\t\tErr       error     // Underlying error for error wrapping\n633\t\tErrorCode ErrorCode // Error code for programmatic handling\n634\t}\n635\t\n636\t// Code implements YAMLError interface.\n637\tfunc (fe *FileError) Code() ErrorCode {\n638\t\tif fe.ErrorCode != \"\" {\n639\t\t\treturn fe.ErrorCode\n640\t\t}\n641\t\treturn ErrCodeFileIOError\n642\t}\n643\t\n644\t// YAMLErrorType implements YAMLError interface.\n645\tfunc (fe *FileError) YAMLErrorType() ErrorType {\n646\t\treturn ErrorTypeFile\n647\t}\n648\t\n649\t// Context implements YAMLError interface.\n650\tfunc (fe *FileError) Context() string {\n651\t\top := fe.Operation\n652\t\tif op == \"\" && fe.Op != \"\" {\n653\t\t\top = fe.Op\n654\t\t}\n655\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"operation: %s, path: %s\", op, fe.Path)\n656\t}\n657\t\n658\t// Error implements the error interface.\n659\tfunc (fe *FileError) Error() string {\n660\t\top := fe.Operation\n661\t\tif op == \"\" && fe.Op != \"\" {\n662\t\t\top = fe.Op\n663\t\t}\n664\t\n665\t\tvar sb strings.Builder\n666\t\tif op != \"\" {\n667\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"file error during %s on %s\", op, fe.Path))\n668\t\t} else {\n669\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"file error in %s\", fe.Path))\n670\t\t}\n671\t\n672\t\tif fe.Message != \"\" {\n673\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\": %s\", fe.Message))\n674\t\t}\n675\t\n676\t\tif fe.Err != nil {\n677\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\": %s\", fe.Err.Error()))\n678\t\t}\n679\t\n680\t\treturn sb.String()\n681\t}\n682\t\n683\t// Unwrap returns the underlying error for error wrapping chains.\n684\tfunc (fe *FileError) Unwrap() error {\n685\t\treturn fe.Err\n686\t}\n687\t\n688\t// IsFileError checks if an error is a FileError.\n689\tfunc IsFileError(err error) bool {\n690\t\tvar fe *FileError\n691\t\treturn err != nil && (errors.As(err, &fe) || isYAMLErrorOfType(err, ErrorTypeFile))\n692\t}\n693\t\n694\t// SyntaxError represents YAML syntax errors during parsing.\n695\t//\n696\t// SyntaxError includes indentation errors, malformed YAML, and other\n697\t// syntax-related issues that prevent the YAML from being parsed.\n698\ttype SyntaxError struct {\n699\t\tFilePath  string    // Path to the file with syntax error\n700\t\tLine      int       // Line number where syntax error occurred\n701\t\tColumn    int       // Column number where syntax error occurred\n702\t\tMessage   string    // Description of the syntax error\n703\t\tExpected  string    // What was expected (if known)\n704\t\tFound     string    // What was actually found (if known)\n705\t\tErr       error     // Underlying yaml parser error\n706\t\tErrorCode ErrorCode // Error code for programmatic handling\n707\t}\n708\t\n709\t// Code implements YAMLError interface.\n710\tfunc (se *SyntaxError) Code() ErrorCode {\n711\t\tif se.ErrorCode != \"\" {\n712\t\t\treturn se.ErrorCode\n713\t\t}\n714\t\treturn ErrCodeInvalidSyntax\n715\t}\n716\t\n717\t// YAMLErrorType implements YAMLError interface.\n718\tfunc (se *SyntaxError) YAMLErrorType() ErrorType {\n719\t\treturn ErrorTypeSyntax\n720\t}\n721\t\n722\t// Context implements YAMLError interface.\n723\tfunc (se *SyntaxError) Context() string {\n724\t\tctx := fmt.Sprintf(\"expected: %s\", se.Expected)\n725\t\tif se.Found != \"\" {\n726\t\t\tctx += fmt.Sprintf(\", found: %s\", se.Found)\n727\t\t}\n728\t\treturn ctx\n729\t}\n730\t\n731\t// Error implements the error interface.\n732\tfunc (se *SyntaxError) Error() string {\n733\t\tif se.Line > 0 {\n734\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"syntax error in %s at line %d, column %d: %s\", se.FilePath, se.Line, se.Column, se.Message)\n735\t\t}\n736\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"syntax error in %s: %s\", se.FilePath, se.Message)\n737\t}\n738\t\n739\t// Unwrap returns the underlying error.\n740\tfunc (se *SyntaxError) Unwrap() error {\n741\t\treturn se.Err\n742\t}\n743\t\n744\t// StructureError represents YAML structure errors during parsing.\n745\t//\n746\t// StructureError includes issues like duplicate keys, invalid nesting,\n747\t// and other *StructureError) Unwrap() error {\n793\t\treturn ste.Err\n794\t}\n795\t\n796\t// TypeMismatchError represents type conversion errors during parsing.\n797\t//\n798\t// TypeMismatchError occurs when indicates a configuration error.\n923\ttype DuplicateKeyError struct {\n924\t\tFilePath  string    // Path to the file with duplicate keys\n925\t\tKey       string    // The duplicate key *DuplicateKeyError) YAMLErrorType() ErrorType {\n942\t\treturn ErrorTypeDuplicateKey\n943\t}\n944\t\n945\t// Context implements YAMLError interface.\n946\tfunc (dke *DuplicateKeyError) Context() dke.Line2 > 0 {\n952\t\t\tctx += fmt.Sprintf(\" (first at line %d, duplicate at line %d)\", dke.Line1, dke.Line2)\n953\t\t}\n954\t\treturn ctx\n955\t}\n956\t\n957\t// Error implements the error interface.\n958\tfunc (dke *DuplicateKeyError) Error() string {\n959\t\tif dke.Line2 > 0 {\n960\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"duplicate key error in %s at line %d: key %q already defined at line %d\",\n961\t\t\t\tdke.FilePath, dke.Line2, dke.Key, dke.Line1)\n962\t\t}\n963\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"duplicate key error in %s: key %q\", dke.FilePath, dke.Key)\n964\t}\n965\t\n966\t// {\n1056\t\t\treturn false\n1057\t\t}\n1058\t\n1059\t\t// Try to unwrap to get to the base YAMLError\n1060\t\tfor unwrapped := err; unwrapped != nil; unwrapped = errors.Unwrap(unwrapped) \"\"\n1093\t}\n1094\t\n1095\t// IsFileNotFoundError checks if an error indicates a file was not found.\n1096\t// This works with both FileError and other wrapped file not found errors.\n1097\tfunc IsFileNotFoundError(err error) bool {\n1098\t\tif err == nil {\n1099\t\t\treturn false\n1100\t\t}\n1101\t\n1102\t\t// Check if error chain contains os.ErrNotExist\n1103\t\tif os.IsNotExist(err) Package yamlutil provides comprehensive error type definitions for YAML processing.\n//\n// Error Hierarchy:\n//\n//\tYAMLError (base interface)\n//\t├── FileError (file I/O errors)\n//\t├── ParseError (YAML parsing errors)\n//\t│   ├── SyntaxError (YAML syntax errors)\n//\t│   ├── StructureError (YAML structure errors)\n//\t│   └── TypeMismatchError (type conversion errors)\n//\t├── ValidationError (validation errors)\n//\t│   ├── FieldNotFoundError (missing required fields)\n//\t│   ├── ConstraintError (constraint violations)\n//\t│   └── DuplicateKeyError (duplicate key errors)\n//\t└── SchemaError (schema-related errors)\n//\t\t├── SchemaLoadError (schema loading errors)\n//\t\t└── SchemaValidationError (schema validation errors)\npackage yamlutil\n\nimport (\n\t\"errors\"\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"os\"\n\t\"strings\"\n)\n\n// YAMLError is the base interface for all YAML processing errors.\n//\n// This interface provides a common type for all errors that occur during\n// YAML file I/O, parsing, validation, and schema operations.\ntype YAMLError interface {\n\terror\n\n\t// Code returns the error code for programmatic error handling.\n\tCode() ErrorCode\n\n\t// YAMLErrorType returns the category of error for type switching.\n\tYAMLErrorType() ErrorType\n\n\t// Context returns additional context about the error.\n\tContext() string\n}\n\n// ErrorType represents the category of YAML error.\ntype ErrorType string\n\nconst (\n\tErrorTypeFile           ErrorType = \"file\"           // File I/O errors\n\tErrorTypeParse          ErrorType = \"parse\"          // YAML parsing errors\n\tErrorTypeSyntax         ErrorType = \"syntax\"         // YAML syntax errors\n\tErrorTypeStructure      ErrorType = \"structure\"      // YAML structure errors\n\tErrorTypeTypeMismatch   ErrorType = \"type_mismatch\"  // Type conversion errors\n\tErrorTypeValidation     ErrorType = \"validation\"     // Validation errors\n\tErrorTypeFieldNotFound  ErrorType = \"field_not_found\" // Missing required fields\n\tErrorTypeConstraint     ErrorType = \"constraint\"     // Constraint violations\n\tErrorTypeDuplicateKey   ErrorType = \"duplicate_key\"  // Duplicate key errors\n\tErrorTypeSchema         ErrorType = \"schema\"         // Schema-related errors\n\tErrorTypeSchemaLoad     ErrorType = \"schema_load\"     // Schema loading errors\n\tErrorTypeSchemaValidate ErrorType = \"schema_validate\" // Schema validation errors\n\tErrorTypeUnknown        ErrorType = \"unknown\"        // Unknown error type\n\tErrorTypeEmpty          ErrorType = \"empty\"          // Empty content\n\tErrorTypeIO             ErrorType = \"io\"             // I/O error\n)\n\n// ParseErrorVariant represents the specific variant/category of a parse error.\n//\n// ParseErrorVariant provides a structured way to categorize different types of\n// parse errors, enabling precise error handling and user-friendly error messages.\n// Each variant represents a distinct category of parsing failure.\ntype ParseErrorVariant string\n\nconst (\n\t// ParseErrorVariantSyntax represents YAML syntax errors.\n\t//\n\t// This variant covers fundamental YAML syntax issues including:\n\t// - Invalid indentation\n\t// - Malformed YAML structure\n\t// - Invalid escape sequences\n\t// - Improper quoting\n\t// - Invalid YAML tokens\n\tParseErrorVariantSyntax ParseErrorVariant = \"syntax\"\n\n\t// ParseErrorVariantTypeMismatch represents type conversion errors.\n\t//\n\t// This variant covers errors when YAML content cannot be converted to the\n\t// expected Go type, including:\n\t// - Attempting to parse a string as an integer\n\t// - Trying to unmarshal a scalar into a struct\n\t// - Incompatible array/slice types\n\t// - Invalid boolean representations\n\tParseErrorVariantTypeMismatch ParseErrorVariant = \"type_mismatch\"\n\n\t// ParseErrorVariantValidation represents constraint validation errors.\n\t//\n\t// This variant covers errors when YAML data violates defined constraints,\n\t// including:\n\t// - Value out of allowed range\n\t// - String length violations\n\t// - Pattern matching failures\n\t// - Required field violations\n\tParseErrorVariantValidation ParseErrorVariant = \"validation\"\n\n\t// ParseErrorVariantIO represents I/O operation errors.\n\t//\n\t// This variant covers errors related to file operations during parsing,\n\t// including:\n\t// - File not found\n\t// - Permission denied\n\t// - Read/write failures\n\t// - File system errors\n\tParseErrorVariantIO ParseErrorVariant = \"io\"\n\n\t// ParseErrorVariantStructure represents YAML structure errors.\n\t//\n\t// This variant covers structural issues that syntax checking alone won't catch,\n\t// including:\n\t// - Duplicate mapping keys\n\t// - Invalid nesting\n\t// - Circular references\n\t// - Inconsistent document structure\n\tParseErrorVariantStructure ParseErrorVariant = \"structure\"\n\n\t// ParseErrorVariantCustom represents custom/extensible error categories.\n\t//\n\t// This variant is reserved for application-specific parsing errors that don't\n\t// fit into the standard categories. It enables extensibility for domain-specific\n\t// validation rules and custom parsing logic.\n\t// Applications can use this variant with custom error codes and messages\n\t// to represent specialized error conditions.\n\tParseErrorVariantCustom ParseErrorVariant = \"custom\"\n)\n\n// String returns the string representation of the ParseErrorVariant.\nfunc (v ParseErrorVariant) String() string {\n\treturn string(v)\n}\n\n// Description returns a human-readable description of the ParseErrorVariant.\nfunc (v ParseErrorVariant) Description() string {\n\tswitch v {\n\tcase ParseErrorVariantSyntax:\n\t\treturn \"YAML syntax error - invalid YAML structure or formatting\"\n\tcase ParseErrorVariantTypeMismatch:\n\t\treturn \"Type mismatch error - cannot convert YAML to expected type\"\n\tcase ParseErrorVariantValidation:\n\t\treturn \"Validation error - constraint violation or invalid value\"\n\tcase ParseErrorVariantIO:\n\t\treturn \"I/O error - file operation failed\"\n\tcase ParseErrorVariantStructure:\n\t\treturn \"Structure error - invalid YAML document structure\"\n\tcase ParseErrorVariantCustom:\n\t\treturn \"Custom error - application-specific parsing error\"\n\tdefault:\n\t\treturn \"Unknown parse error variant\"\n\t}\n}\n\n// ErrorCode represents specific error codes for programmatic error handling.\n//\n// ErrorCodes provide machine-readable identifiers for errors, enabling\n// programmatic error handling and internationalization of error messages.\ntype ErrorCode string\n\nconst (\n\t// File error codes\n\tErrCodeFileNotFound      ErrorCode = \"FILE_NOT_FOUND\"      // File does not exist\n\tErrCodeFileAccessDenied ErrorCode = \"FILE_ACCESS_DENIED\" // Permission denied\n\tErrCodeFileIOError       ErrorCode = \"FILE_IO_ERROR\"      // Generic I/O error\n\tErrCodeFileEmpty         ErrorCode = \"FILE_EMPTY\"         // File is empty\n\n\t// Parse error codes\n\tErrCodeInvalidSyntax   ErrorCode = \"INVALID_SYNTAX\"    // YAML syntax error\n\tErrCodeTypeMismatch    ErrorCode = \"TYPE_MISMATCH\"    // Type conversion error\n\tErrCodeInvalidStructure ErrorCode = \"INVALID_STRUCTURE\" // YAML structure error\n\tErrCodeDuplicateKey    ErrorCode = \"DUPLICATE_KEY\"    // Duplicate mapping key\n\tErrCodeParseError       ErrorCode = \"PARSE_ERROR\"      // Generic parse error\n\n\t// Validation error codes\n\tErrCodeValidationFailed   ErrorCode = \"VALIDATION_FAILED\"    // Validation failed\n\tErrCodeRequiredField      ErrorCode = \"REQUIRED_FIELD\"       // Missing required field\n\tErrCodeConstraintViolation ErrorCode = \"CONSTRAINT_VIOLATION\" // Constraint violated\n\tErrCodeInvalidValue       ErrorCode = \"INVALID_VALUE\"        // Invalid value\n\n\t// Schema error codes\n\tErrCodeSchemaLoadFailed   ErrorCode = \"SCHEMA_LOAD_FAILED\"    // Schema loading failed\n\tErrCodeSchemaValidation   ErrorCode = \"SCHEMA_VALIDATION\"    // Schema validation failed\n\tErrCodeSchemaNotFound     ErrorCode = \"SCHEMA_NOT_FOUND\"      // Schema not found\n\tErrCodeSchemaInvalid      ErrorCode = \"SCHEMA_INVALID\"         // Invalid schema definition\n)\n\n// ParseError represents errors that occur during YAML parsing.\n//\n// ParseError wraps underlying parsing errors and provides detailed\n// location information (line, column) and context about what failed.\ntype ParseError struct {\n\tFilePath   string      // Path to the file being parsed\n\tLine       int         // Line number where error occurred (1-indexed)\n\tColumn     int         // Column number where error occurred (1-indexed)\n\tMessage    string      // Human-readable error message\n\tContextStr string      // Additional context about the parsing state\n\tErr        error       // Underlying error for error wrapping\n\tErrorType  ErrorType   // Specific type of parse error\n\tErrorCode  ErrorCode   // Error code for programmatic handling\n\tExpected   string      // What was expected (optional, for syntax/type errors)\n\tActual     string      // What was actually found (optional, for syntax/type errors)\n}\n\n// Code implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (pe *ParseError) Code() ErrorCode {\n\tif pe.ErrorCode != \"\" {\n\t\treturn pe.ErrorCode\n\t}\n\treturn ErrCodeParseError\n}\n\n// YAMLErrorType implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (pe *ParseError) YAMLErrorType() ErrorType {\n\treturn pe.ErrorType\n}\n\n// Context implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (pe *ParseError) Context() string {\n\treturn pe.ContextStr\n}\n\n// Error implements the error interface.\nfunc (pe *ParseError) Error() string {\n\tvar sb strings.Builder\n\n\t// Build base error with location\n\tif pe.Line > 0 {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"parse error in %s at line %d\", pe.FilePath, pe.Line))\n\t\tif pe.Column > 0 {\n\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\", column %d\", pe.Column))\n\t\t}\n\t} else {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"parse error in %s\", pe.FilePath))\n\t}\n\n\t// Add message\n\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\": %s\", pe.Message))\n\n\t// Add expected vs actual if available\n\tif pe.Expected != \"\" || pe.Actual != \"\" {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(\" (\")\n\t\tif pe.Expected != \"\" {\n\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"expected: %s\", pe.Expected))\n\t\t}\n\t\tif pe.Expected != \"\" && pe.Actual != \"\" {\n\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\", \")\n\t\t}\n\t\tif pe.Actual != \"\" {\n\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"actual: %s\", pe.Actual))\n\t\t}\n\t\tsb.WriteString(\")\")\n\t}\n\n\treturn sb.String()\n}\n\n// Unwrap returns the underlying error for error wrapping chains.\nfunc (pe *ParseError) Unwrap() error {\n\treturn pe.Err\n}\n\n// NewParseError creates a new ParseError with the given parameters.\n//\n// This constructor function provides a convenient way to create properly initialized\n// ParseError instances with message, line, column, error code, and optional expected/actual values.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - filePath: Path to the file being parsed\n//   - message: Human-readable error message\n//   - line: Line number where error occurred (1-indexed, use 0 if unknown)\n//   - column: Column number where error occurred (1-indexed, use 0 if unknown)\n//   - code: Error code for programmatic handling (use empty string for default)\n//   - expected: What was expected (optional, use empty string if not applicable)\n//   - actual: What was actually found (optional, use empty string if not applicable)\n//\n// Returns a properly initialized ParseError that implements the YAMLError interface.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\terr := NewParseError(\"config.yaml\", \"invalid syntax\", 10, 5, ErrCodeInvalidSyntax, \"identifier\", \"123\")\nfunc NewParseError(filePath string, message string, line int, column int, code ErrorCode, expected string, actual string) *ParseError {\n\t// Use provided code or default to generic parse error\n\terrorCode := code\n\tif errorCode == \"\" {\n\t\terrorCode = ErrCodeParseError\n\t}\n\n\treturn &ParseError{\n\t\tFilePath:  filePath,\n\t\tMessage:   message,\n\t\tLine:      line,\n\t\tColumn:    column,\n\t\tErrorCode: errorCode,\n\t\tErrorType: ErrorTypeParse,\n\t\tExpected:  expected,\n\t\tActual:    actual,\n\t}\n}\n\n// IsParseError checks if an error is a ParseError.\nfunc IsParseError(err error) bool {\n\tvar pe *ParseError\n\treturn err != nil && (errors.As(err, &pe) || isYAMLErrorOfType(err, ErrorTypeParse))\n}\n\n// ValidationError represents errors that occur during YAML validation.\n//\n// ValidationError wraps validation failures and provides detailed\n// information about what failed validation. This is the base error type\n// used throughout the yamlutil package for validation errors.\ntype ValidationError struct {\n\tFilePath   string    // Path to the file being validated\n\tFieldPath  string    // Dot-notation path to the invalid field (optional)\n\tMessage    string    // Human-readable error message\n\tLine       int       // Line number where error occurred (1-indexed)\n\tColumn     int       // Column number where error occurred (1-indexed, optional)\n\tConstraint string    // Constraint that was violated (optional)\n\tContextStr string    // Additional context about the validation state (optional)\n\tErr        error     // Underlying error for error wrapping (optional)\n\tErrorCode  ErrorCode // Error code for programmatic handling (optional)\n\tType       ErrorType // Category of error for type switching\n}\n\n// Code implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (ve *ValidationError) Code() ErrorCode {\n\tif ve.ErrorCode != \"\" {\n\t\treturn ve.ErrorCode\n\t}\n\treturn ErrCodeValidationFailed\n}\n\n// YAMLErrorType implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (ve *ValidationError) YAMLErrorType() ErrorType {\n\t// If Type is explicitly set, use it\n\tif ve.Type != \"\" {\n\t\treturn ve.Type\n\t}\n\t// Try to determine error type from error code or message\n\tif ve.ErrorCode == ErrCodeRequiredField || (ve.FieldPath != \"\" && containsRequiredFieldKeywords(ve.Message)) {\n\t\treturn ErrorTypeFieldNotFound\n\t}\n\tif ve.ErrorCode == ErrCodeConstraintViolation || containsConstraintKeywords(ve.Message) {\n\t\treturn ErrorTypeConstraint\n\t}\n\treturn ErrorTypeValidation\n}\n\n// Context implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (ve *ValidationError) Context() string {\n\treturn ve.ContextStr\n}\n\n// Error implements the error interface.\nfunc (ve *ValidationError) Error() string {\n\tvar sb strings.Builder\n\n\t// Build base error with location\n\tif ve.Line > 0 {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"validation error in %s at line %d\", ve.FilePath, ve.Line))\n\t\tif ve.Column > 0 {\n\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\", column %d\", ve.Column))\n\t\t}\n\t} else {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"validation error in %s\", ve.FilePath))\n\t}\n\n\t// Add field path if available\n\tif ve.FieldPath != \"\" {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" at field %s\", ve.FieldPath))\n\t}\n\n\t// Add message\n\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\": %s\", ve.Message))\n\n\t// Add constraint if available\n\tif ve.Constraint != \"\" {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" (constraint: %s)\", ve.Constraint))\n\t}\n\n\treturn sb.String()\n}\n\n// Unwrap returns the underlying error for error wrapping chains.\nfunc (ve *ValidationError) Unwrap() error {\n\treturn ve.Err\n}\n\n// String returns a formatted error message with context.\nfunc (ve *ValidationError) String() string {\n\tvar sb strings.Builder\n\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Error: %s\\n\", ve.Message))\n\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Type: %s\\n\", ve.YAMLErrorType()))\n\tif ve.Line > 0 {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Location: Line %d\", ve.Line))\n\t\tif ve.Column > 0 {\n\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\", Column %d\", ve.Column))\n\t\t}\n\t\tsb.WriteString(\"\\n\")\n\t}\n\tif ve.FieldPath != \"\" {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Field: %s\\n\", ve.FieldPath))\n\t}\n\tif ve.Constraint != \"\" {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Constraint: %s\\n\", ve.Constraint))\n\t}\n\tif ve.ContextStr != \"\" {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Context: %s\\n\", ve.ContextStr))\n\t}\n\treturn sb.String()\n}\n\n// NewValidationError creates a new ValidationError with the given parameters.\n//\n// This constructor function provides a - message: Human-readable error message\n//   - fieldPath: Dot-notation path to the invalid field (optional)\n//   - constraint: Constraint that was violated (optional)\n//   - code: Error code for errorCode,\n\t\tType:       ErrorTypeValidation,\n\t}\n}\n\n// IsValidationError checks if an error is a ValidationError.\nfunc IsValidationError(err error) bool {\n\tvar ve *ValidationError\n\treturn
